mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in consumer credit outstanding (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal synchronized demand-pull inflation and robust economic momentum, which compresses equity risk premiums and lifts forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where industrial production peaks, supply chain bottlenecks reverse and capacity utilization normalizes, causing profit margins to compress and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 65% of late-cycle quarters where INDPRO growth decelerates, invalidating the margin compression thesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects rising business and consumer confidence, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative or zero directional accuracy over a 5-year walk-forward test, or housing starts rise while equity returns fall in more than 70% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, borrowing costs suppress corporate leverage and equity valuations, triggering a mean-reversion dynamic that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in > 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and a stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a healthy labor market without overheating, which supports steady earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample months, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market contraction phase, declining housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probabilities, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where HOUST declines, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in a 5-year out-of-sample test.
